faqs.question: What pantry pests are most common in Cypress Hills homes?
faqs.answer: Indian meal moths, flour beetles, cigarette beetles, and grain weevils are the most frequent pantry pests in Cypress Hills. These pests infest dry goods like flour, cereal, pasta, rice, nuts, and dried fruits, often arriving in contaminated packages from stores or spreading between units in multi-family buildings.

faqs.question: How quickly can you provide pantry pest control service in Cypress Hills?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day and next-day service to Cypress Hills residents. Our technicians can typically arrive within 24 hours of your call to inspect your pantry, identify the infestation source, and begin treatment immediately to prevent further contamination of your food supplies.

faqs.question: What does the $350-$550 pantry pest control service include?
faqs.answer: Our service includes a thorough inspection of all food storage areas, identification of infested products, targeted treatment of cabinets and pantry spaces, crack and crevice application in problem areas, pheromone traps for monitoring, and detailed prevention recommendations. Pricing varies based on infestation severity and treatment area size.

faqs.question: How can I prevent pantry pests from returning after treatment?
faqs.answer: Store dry goods in airtight glass or plastic containers, inspect groceries before storing them, clean spills immediately, rotate older products to the front, and regularly check expiration dates. We'll provide specific guidance during our visit and can schedule follow-up inspections to ensure your Cypress Hills kitchen remains pest-free.
